metadata.title: The Fluency Trap: Smooth Is Not Correct
metadata.description_blurb: A smooth, confident Claude output is not evidence of a correct one — fluency is a training artifact, not an accuracy signal, and asking Claude to check itself is not an independent check.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: She pasted a citation into Claude and asked: is this accurate? Claude returned a confident paragraph explaining what the paper argued and why it was relevant. She submitted the paper. The citation does not exist. The paper does not exist. Claude did not check the citation — it generated a plausible account of what the citation would have argued if the paper had existed. The output was confident. The confidence was a training artifact. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, asking Claude to verify a citation

beats.narration_text: Fluency is a training artifact. Claude is trained to produce confident, human-like text at scale. High-entropy outputs — guesses, fabrications, plausible-sounding claims without source — match the fluency of low-entropy outputs — verified facts, direct quotations. Farquhar 2024's semantic entropy research shows you cannot distinguish them from surface features. A hallucination reads like a true statement because both are generated by the same process.
beats.new_visual_element: Fluency not accuracy — training artifact / Farquhar 2024 / hallucination ≡ truth at the surface / same process

beats.narration_text: Asking Claude to check its own output is not an independent check. The same weights that generated the original claim evaluate whether it sounds correct. If the weights were wrong the first time, they are wrong the second time. There is no independent source being consulted. 'Claude said it reviewed the sources' is one of the six false checks — alongside 'sounds right,' 'Claude cited it,' 'table looks clean,' 'chart is beautiful,' and 'test passed.'
beats.new_visual_element: Self-check not independent — same weights / six false checks / sounds right / cited it / table clean / chart beautiful / test passed

beats.narration_text: The rule is one sentence: no verification path, no delegation. Define what evidence would confirm the output before you delegate the task. For a citation: open the source, confirm title and author, check the paper says what Claude claims. For a number: recompute it from the source data. For a claim: find the primary source it depends on. If you cannot define the evidence, you cannot verify the output. If you cannot verify it, you own the error.
beats.new_visual_element: One rule — no verification path no delegation / define evidence before delegating / citation / number / claim checks

beats.narration_text: Fluency literacy is not about distrust. It is about calibration. Claude is excellent at synthesis, drafting, organizing, and generating plausible structure. It is not a fact-checking tool. The verification step — opening the source, recomputing the number, confirming the citation — is not a workaround for a broken system. It is the correct use of the system. Claude generates. You verify. That division is the model.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — Claude generates / you verify / correct division of labor / fluency literacy = calibration not distrust

beats.narration_text: Name your verification path before the next delegation. Paste this.

props.command: Before you generate: tell me what type of claims this output will make — factual, citation, numerical, synthesis. For each type, tell me what I would need to check to verify it is correct. Then generate the output. I will use your list as my verification checklist. If any claim cannot be verified with a primary source I can open, flag it as [unverifiable] in the output.

beats.narration_text: If this keeps a fluent fabrication out of a submitted document, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
